testcase.name: test_fetch_gists

failure.message: aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host api.github.com:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)')]

testcase.failure:
self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x1082d7310>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x1082e0b10> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'api.github.com', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x107f81880>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout > return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock)
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1313: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/base_events.py:1112: in create_connection transport, protocol = await self._create_connection_transport( /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/base_events.py:1145: in _create_connection_transport await waiter /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/sslproto.py:575: in _on_handshake_complete raise handshake_exc /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/asyncio/sslproto.py:557: in _do_handshake self._sslobj.do_handshake() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <ssl.SSLObject object at 0x105273490>
def do_handshake(self): """Start the SSL/TLS handshake.""" > self._sslobj.do_handshake() E ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/ssl.py:979: SSLCertVerificationError
The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4431374288'>
@pytest.mark.asyncio @patch("requests.get") async def test_fetch_gists(mock_get): mock_response = MagicMock() mock_response.json.return_value = [example_gist_data] mock_get.return_value = mock_response > gists = await fetch_gists()
tests/test_utils.py:41: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ scripts/utils.py:117: in fetch_gists async with session.get(GITHUB_API, headers=HEADERS) as response: .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:1510: in __aenter__ self._resp: _RetType = await self._coro .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:779: in _request resp = await handler(req) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:734: in _connect_and_send_request conn = await self._connector.connect( .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:672: in connect proto = await self._create_connection(req, traces, timeout) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1239: in _create_connection _, proto = await self._create_direct_connection(req, traces, timeout) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1611: in _create_direct_connection raise last_exc .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1580: in _create_direct_connection transp, proto = await self._wrap_create_connection( _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x1082d7310>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x1082e0b10> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'api.github.com', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x107f81880>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) except cert_errors as exc: > raise ClientConnectorCertificateError(req.connection_key, exc) from exc E aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host api.github.com:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)')]

testcase.name: test_fetch_gist_content

failure.message: aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host gist.githubusercontent.com:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)')]

self = <aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector object at 0x108444590>, addr_infos = [] req = <aiohttp.client_reqrep.ClientRequest object at 0x1084458d0> timeout = ClientTimeout(total=300, connect=None, sock_read=None, sock_connect=30, ceil_threshold=5) client_error = <class 'aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorError'> args = (functools.partial(<class 'aiohttp.client_proto.ResponseHandler'>, loop=<_UnixSelectorEventLoop running=False closed=False debug=False>),) kwargs = {'server_hostname': 'gist.githubusercontent.com', 'ssl': <ssl.SSLContext object at 0x107f81880>} sock = <socket.socket [closed] fd=-1, family=2, type=1, proto=6>
async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout > return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock)

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:
mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4433721744'>
@pytest.mark.asyncio @patch("requests.get") async def test_fetch_gist_content(mock_get): mock_response = MagicMock() mock_response.text = "print('Hello, world!')" mock_get.return_value = mock_response > content = await fetch_gist_content( "https://gist.githubusercontent.com/rjvitorino/dfef5433066c061954d29d1b15202290/raw/54a0207aaf64e70e57a2063795b0a1c88a6b046c/file1.py" )
tests/test_utils.py:52: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ scripts/utils.py:141: in fetch_gist_content async with session.get(raw_url) as response: .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:1510: in __aenter__ self._resp: _RetType = await self._coro .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:779: in _request resp = await handler(req) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py:734: in _connect_and_send_request conn = await self._connector.connect( .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:672: in connect proto = await self._create_connection(req, traces, timeout) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1239: in _create_connection _, proto = await self._create_direct_connection(req, traces, timeout) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1611: in _create_direct_connection raise last_exc .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py:1580: in _create_direct_connection transp, proto = await self._wrap_create_connection( _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

async def _wrap_create_connection( self, *args: Any, addr_infos: List[AddrInfoType], req: ClientRequest, timeout: "ClientTimeout", client_error: Type[Exception] = ClientConnectorError, **kwargs: Any, ) -> Tuple[asyncio.Transport, ResponseHandler]: try: async with ceil_timeout( timeout.sock_connect, ceil_threshold=timeout.ceil_threshold ): sock = await aiohappyeyeballs.start_connection( addr_infos=addr_infos, local_addr_infos=self._local_addr_infos, happy_eyeballs_delay=self._happy_eyeballs_delay, interleave=self._interleave, loop=self._loop, socket_factory=self._socket_factory, ) # Add ssl_shutdown_timeout for Python 3.11+ when SSL is used if ( kwargs.get("ssl") and self._ssl_shutdown_timeout and sys.version_info >= (3, 11) ): kwargs["ssl_shutdown_timeout"] = self._ssl_shutdown_timeout return await self._loop.create_connection(*args, **kwargs, sock=sock) except cert_errors as exc: > raise ClientConnectorCertificateError(req.connection_key, exc) from exc E aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientConnectorCertificateError: Cannot connect to host gist.githubusercontent.com:443 ssl:True [SSLCertVerificationError: (1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1006)')]

testcase.name: test_create_gist_index

failure.message: TypeError: Value after * must be an iterable, not async_generator

testcase.failure:
mock_file = <MagicMock name='open' id='4432525648'>
@pytest.mark.asyncio @patch("builtins.open", new_callable=mock_open) async def test_create_gist_index(mock_file): > await create_gist_index(example_gist)
tests/test_utils.py:82: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
gist = Gist(url='', forks_url='', commits_url='', id='1', node_id='', git_pull_url='', git_push_url='', html_url='', files={'...mments=0, comments_url='', owner={'login': 'rjvitorino', 'html_url': 'https://github.com/rjvitorino'}, truncated=False)
async def create_gist_index(gist: Gist) -> str: """ Create or update the index.md file for a gist.
Args: gist (Gist): The Gist object.
Returns: folder_name (str): The folder name of the gist """ folder_name = await generate_folder_name(gist) gist_dir = Path("gists") / folder_name gist_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
> index_content = [ f"# {folder_name}", "", f"**Gist file**: [{gist.html_url}]({gist.html_url})", "", f"**Description**: {gist.description}", "", *( f"## {filename}\n\n```{fileinfo.language}\n{await fetch_gist_content(fileinfo.raw_url)}\n```" for filename, fileinfo in gist.files.items() ), ] E TypeError: Value after * must be an iterable, not async_generator
scripts/utils.py:251: TypeError
